schema.title: original Q
schema.config_params: list of related IoTDB config parameters
schema.anomaly_classes: subset of {compaction, export, flush, full_cpu, full_memory, network_bandwidth2}
schema.iotdb_subsystems: subset of {Flush, Compaction, MemTable, TsFile, WAL, Memory, Network, Compression, Query, Cache, Procedure, Schema, GC, Election}

tuples.title: How to deal with estimated out of memory errors?
tuples.symptom: Error 301: There is not enough memory to execute current fragment instance, current remaining free memory is X, estimated memory usage for current fragment instance is Y
tuples.root_cause: Memory available for query execution (heap × datanode_memory_proportion × chunk_timeseriesmeta_free_memory_proportion = 30% × 20% = 6% of heap) is less than the query's estimated execution memory requirement. By default a 4G heap leaves only ~240MB for query execution.
fix: Increase IOTDB_HEAP_SIZE to >4.2G (so 4.2G * 30% * 20% > 257MB)
Tune datanode_memory_proportion to allocate more memory to queries
Reduce the number of exported time series in the query
Add 'slimit N' clause to limit number of timeseries per query
Add 'align by device' to reduce memory to single-device level

tuples.title: How to handle IndexOutOfBoundsException from GrpcLogAppender?
tuples.root_cause: Internal Ratis 2.4.1 library bug; reported to Ratis community.
fix: No action needed; does not affect data writes/reads. Will be fixed in future IoTDB releases that upgrade Ratis.

tuples.title: Resolving lsof Issues in IoTDB Deployment
tuples.symptom: lsof command missing or version incompatibility during IoTDB deployment
tuples.root_cause: Wrong lsof version or missing /bin/lsof binary; IoTDB uses lsof for port detection at startup.
fix: Backup existing lsof: sudo mv /bin/lsof /bin/lsof.bak
Install RPM package matching OS version: sudo rpm -ivh lsof-4.87-6.el7.x86_64.rpm
Verify with: lsof -v
Create symlink if needed: sudo ln -sf $(which lsof) /bin/lsof

tuples.title: Failed to start ConfigNode for the first time
tuples.symptom: ConfigNode startup fails on first deployment
tuples.root_cause: Possible: (1) data/confignode directory not empty, (2) port conflict, (3) cn_seed_config_node misconfigured (should point to itself on first start), (4) consensus/replica config mismatch with seed node.
fix: Clear data/confignode directory on first start
Check IP+Port availability
Set cn_seed_config_node to self on first ConfigNode of cluster
Ensure consensus protocol and replica number match across ConfigNodes

tuples.title: ConfigNode started but missing from 'show cluster'
tuples.symptom: ConfigNode process is alive but does not appear in 'show cluster' output
tuples.root_cause: cn_seed_config_node points to itself instead of an existing ConfigNode, accidentally creating a separate new cluster.
fix: Stop the ConfigNode and reconfigure cn_seed_config_node to point to an existing alive ConfigNode in the target cluster.

tuples.title: Failed to start DataNode for the first time
tuples.symptom: DataNode startup fails, possibly with 'Reject DataNode restart' error
tuples.root_cause: Possible: (1) data/datanode directory not empty, (2) port conflict, (3) dn_seed_config_node not pointing to alive ConfigNode.
fix: Clear data/datanode directory on first start
Check IP+Port not conflicting
Ensure dn_seed_config_node points to alive ConfigNode

tuples.title: Failed to remove DataNode
tuples.symptom: remove-datanode.sh fails or DataNode stuck in 'Removing' state
tuples.root_cause: Possible: (1) wrong arguments to remove-datanode.sh, (2) cluster has too few DataNodes (must be > max(schema_replication_factor, data_replication_factor)), (3) region migration failure leaves DataNode in 'Removing' state forever.
fix: Use correct arguments: rpcIp:rpcPort and dataNodeId only
Ensure number of alive DataNodes > max replica factor before removal
Run 'set system status to running' to bring stuck DataNode back
Use 'migrate region from datanodeId1 to datanodeId2' to migrate stuck regions
Future: use 'remove-datanode.sh -f' for forced removal (discards failed regions)

tuples.title: Whether down DataNode can be removed
tuples.symptom: DataNode is down/unreachable, need to remove it permanently
tuples.root_cause: Down DataNode removal requires data redundancy. If replica factor = 1, data on that node has no copy elsewhere.
fix: Only possible if schema/data replica factor > 1. Future: 'remove-datanode.sh -f' option.

tuples.title: Upgrade from 0.13 to 1.0
tuples.symptom: Need to migrate data from IoTDB 0.13 to 1.0
tuples.root_cause: File structure differs significantly between 0.13 and 1.0; data directories not directly compatible. Default RPC address also changed (0.0.0.0 → 127.0.0.1).
fix: Use LOAD function to load 0.13 data into 1.0 (not direct copy)
Update RPC address config if accessing from external clients (set rpc_address back to 0.0.0.0 if needed)

tuples.title: Cannot restart removed ConfigNode/DataNode with old data dir
tuples.symptom: Error: 'Reject ConfigNode/DataNode restart. Because there are no corresponding ConfigNode/DataNode (whose nodeId=xx) in the cluster.'
tuples.root_cause: After removal, the cluster purges the nodeId from its metadata. Restarting with the same data directory creates a stale-id conflict.
fix: Cannot reuse old data directory after node removal. Need to clear data dir and rejoin as a fresh node.

tuples.title: Cannot start node if process not killed
tuples.symptom: Error: 'The port is already occupied' when running start-confignode.sh/start-datanode.sh after deleting data dir
tuples.root_cause: The old IoTDB process is still running and holding the RPC port.
fix: Kill PID first: stop-confignode.sh / stop-datanode.sh or kill <PID>, then start.

tuples.title: 'show cluster' failed with 'please check server status'
tuples.symptom: show cluster command fails; logs mention 'please check server status'
tuples.root_cause: Possible: (1) more than half ConfigNodes are down (no Raft quorum), (2) the DataNode the client connects to is down.
fix: Ensure > N/2 ConfigNodes are alive (Raft quorum requirement)
Switch client to connect to an alive DataNode

tuples.title: How to fix DataNode with broken disk file
tuples.symptom: DataNode reports disk file corruption / read errors
tuples.root_cause: Local disk file (TsFile, WAL, schema) corrupted due to disk failure or OS-level error.
fix: Use remove-datanode.sh to remove broken DataNode (data is migrated to alive replicas)
Replace disk, then add the node back as a fresh DataNode
Future: Node-Fix tool

tuples.title: How to decrease memory usage of ConfigNode/DataNode
tuples.symptom: Node consuming too much memory; OOM risk; need to fit smaller hardware
tuples.root_cause: Default JVM heap/off-heap sizes set for typical workloads; may exceed available physical memory on small machines.
fix: Reduce ON_HEAP_MEMORY and OFF_HEAP_MEMORY in conf/confignode-env.sh and conf/datanode-env.sh.
